pattern: A dynamic program keyed on the last value placed, advanced position by position, balances keeping the original entry against the cheapest larger replacement from the sorted spare array.
hints: At each index you either keep the original value (only if it stays above the previous value) or replace it with something from the second array; track the best cost for each possible 'previous value'.
Sort and dedup the replacement array so you can binary-search the smallest value strictly greater than the current previous value when you need a replacement.
Carry a map from last-placed-value to minimum operations; for each new element, branch on keep versus replace-with-next-bigger, and the answer is the min cost over all final states (or -1 if none).

edge_inputs.description: duplicates in arr2 do not help twice
must replace the first to enable the rest

stress.description: moderate arrays over a small value range so many feasible replacements exist
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    m = max(1, min(size, 60))
    arr1 = [rng.randint(0, 40) for _ in range(n)]
    arr2 = [rng.randint(0, 40) for _ in range(m)]
    return [arr1, arr2]
stress.note: small range keeps the exponential oracle tractable while exercising the DP
